features.name: Customer Types
features.description: Customers are classified into four types: new (Neukunde), regular (Stammkunde), supplier (Lieferant), property_management (Hausverwaltung). NULL customer_type is treated as 'new' in list counts and CSV export.
risks: No DB-level CHECK constraint on customer_type values — only application-level enforcement

features.name: Phone Deduplication (Mobile vs Landline)
features.description: German mobile numbers (+49 15x/16x/17x) are treated as uniquely owned by one person and deduplicate on the number alone. Landline/unknown numbers require name confirmation to be considered a duplicate. This logic is shared by all three insert paths: manual create API, AI agent path (get_or_create_customer), and CSV import.
risks: Legacy rows stored before E.164 normalization may not match on dedup until backfilled

features.name: Phone Normalization to E.164
features.description: All phone numbers are normalized to E.164 format before storage and before dedup lookup. The _to_e164 function handles: already E.164, local German 0-prefix (trunk drop + +49 prepend), 00-prefix international, and international without +.
risks: Default country code is DE (+49); non-German numbers classified as 'unknown' and treated landline-like for dedup

features.name: Customer Number Auto-Generation
features.description: System-generated customer numbers use the KI-NNNNNN format (6-digit zero-padded, per-org sequence). The KI- prefix prevents collision with operator's own numbering schemes. CSV imports may retain their own numbers verbatim. Manual creation allows override.
risks: gen_customer_number reads all existing KI- numbers to find max — expensive on large orgs (must page via fetch_all_rows)

features.name: Soft Delete
features.description: Customers are never hard-deleted. Setting status='deleted' soft-deletes them; all list/dedup queries filter .neq('status','deleted'). Bulk delete via POST /api/customers/bulk-delete also uses soft delete.
risks: Deleted customers are invisible to dedup — same phone/email could be re-created as a new customer after soft-delete

features.name: CSV Import with Content-Aware Column Detection
features.description: CSV import detects column types from cell data (not headers) via _value_type. A 'Mail' column that actually holds phone numbers is detected and the phone value is salvaged into phone or phone2. Addresses stranded in notes columns are extracted via PLZ-anchored regex. Encoding tries UTF-8-sig, UTF-8, cp1252, latin-1 in order.
risks: Junk email detection relies on regex; unusual addresses could be misclassified

features.name: AI Agent Customer Identification
features.description: ElevenLabs AI agent identifies customers via 4 lookup strategies in priority order: (1) customer_number exact match, (2) address or last_name fuzzy search, (3) explicit phoneNumber, (4) caller_number (Caller-ID). Detects forwarded calls where Caller-ID matches the org's own phone number.
risks: Phone lookup only matches the primary phone column (not phone2) for the identify tool; dedup uses both columns

features.name: Secondary Phone Number (phone2)
features.description: Customers have a second phone field (phone2) for mobile/alternate numbers. During get_or_create_customer, if a known-name customer calls from a new number (single exact name match), the new number is stored as phone2 rather than creating a duplicate record. Dedup checks both phone and phone2 columns.
risks: phone2 is silently overwritten each time a known customer calls from a new number — only the most recent alternate number is retained

features.name: Address Storage (Two Shapes)
features.description: Address is stored as JSONB in two possible shapes: {raw: string} for manually entered addresses, and {street, postal_code, city} for CSV-imported addresses. A generated column address_text flattens both shapes for sorting (migration 0051). Frontend handles both shapes client-side.
risks: Two address shapes increase client complexity; no migration to unify legacy raw vs structured data

features.name: Org-Scoped Multi-Tenancy
features.description: All customer CRUD operations filter by org_id from the authenticated user's JWT. RLS policies also enforce org isolation at the DB layer. The backend uses service-role key (bypasses RLS) but always passes org_id in queries. AI tool webhooks resolve org_id from the X-HeyKiki-Secret header or _agentId body field.

rules.name: Customer Dedup: Mobile Phone Is Unique
rules.description: A German mobile number (+49 15x, 16x, or 17x) is treated as uniquely owned by one person. If an exact E.164 match is found in either the phone or phone2 column of any non-deleted customer in the org, the existing record is returned without creating a new one.
rules.purpose: Prevent duplicate customer records when a known customer calls from the same mobile they registered with.
rules.trigger: Any customer create path: POST /api/customers (manual), get_or_create_customer (AI/post-call), POST /api/customers/import (CSV)
validations: Phone must normalize to E.164 via _to_e164
classify_phone must return 'mobile'
actions: Normalize phone to E.164
Query customers table for org_id + (phone=E.164 OR phone2=E.164) + status!=deleted
If any match found, return first match
failureConditions: Phone is not normalizable (empty/non-digit) — falls through to name or create
rules.evidence: classify_phone(phone_norm) == 'mobile' → if rows: return rows[0] (customers.py:61-64). classify_phone: e164.startswith('+49') and national[:2] in ('15','16','17') → 'mobile' (csv_import.py:108-122)

rules.name: Customer Dedup: Landline Requires Name Confirmation
rules.description: A landline or non-German (unknown) phone number deduplicates only when both the phone AND the caller's name match an existing customer. Without a name, a landline phone match alone is treated as a duplicate (to preserve pre-helper call-path behavior). A non-German number is always classified as 'unknown' and treated landline-like.
rules.purpose: A landline phone can be shared (couple, property management office) so phone alone is insufficient to identify a unique person.
rules.trigger: Any customer create path where phone does not match mobile classification
preconditions: Phone is NOT a DE mobile (classify_phone returns 'landline' or 'unknown')
validations: Phone must normalize to E.164
Name comparison is case-insensitive (casefold)
actions: Find rows matching phone or phone2 in org
If name provided: check if any row's full_name casefolded equals name casefolded; return match or None
If no name: return first phone match (fallback to preserve agent path behavior)
failureConditions: Name provided but does not match any phone-matched row → returns None (new customer)
rules.evidence: elif rows: if name: ... match = next((r for r in rows if casefold match), None); if match: return match. else: return rows[0] (customers.py:65-84)

rules.name: Customer Dedup: Email Match
rules.description: If an email address is provided, it is checked first (before phone) against existing non-deleted customers in the org. Email comparison tries both the provided casing and the lowercase form to handle legacy rows stored with mixed case. An email match alone is sufficient to identify a duplicate.
rules.purpose: Email addresses are globally unique to one person so an exact email match is a reliable dedup signal.
rules.trigger: Any customer create path where email is provided
validations: Email is stripped of whitespace before comparison
Both original-case and lowercased versions are tried
actions: For each of (email.strip(), email.strip().lower()) deduplicated: query customers for org + email match + status!=deleted
If match found, return it immediately (email check has highest priority)
failureConditions: Email not provided — skip to phone check
rules.evidence: if email: for cand in dict.fromkeys([email.strip(), email.strip().lower()]): rows = _q().eq('email', cand).limit(1).execute().data; if rows: return rows[0] (customers.py:45-53)

rules.name: Customer Dedup: Name-Only Fallback
rules.description: When no phone is provided (e.g., an email-only contact), dedup falls back to exact full_name matching. If a customer with the same name exists in the org, the existing record is returned rather than creating a duplicate.
rules.purpose: Prevent duplicate records when retrying a create without a phone number.
rules.trigger: Customer create path with no normalizable phone but a name is provided
validations: Name match is exact (case-sensitive, stored value)
actions: Query customers.full_name = name within org and status!=deleted
Return first match if found
failureConditions: Name differs by case from stored value → no match, new customer created
rules.evidence: elif name: rows = _q().eq('full_name', name).limit(1).execute().data; if rows: return rows[0] (customers.py:81-84). NOTE: no casefold here unlike the phone+name path — case-sensitivity is a silent inconsistency.

rules.name: Known Customer Calling From New Number → phone2 Attachment
rules.description: If a new, unrecognized phone number is provided, but there is exactly ONE existing non-deleted customer with the same full_name (case-insensitive), the new number is stored as phone2 on that customer instead of creating a duplicate. If there are 0 or 2+ name matches, a new customer record is created.
rules.purpose: Handle the case where a known customer calls from a new SIM, work phone, or borrowed handset without creating a duplicate row.
rules.trigger: get_or_create_customer: find_existing_customer returned None, phone is provided, name is provided
validations: Name is matched case-insensitively via ilike (no wildcards — exact, case-insensitive)
Exactly 1 same-name customer must exist (not 0, not 2+)
New phone must not already be stored as phone or phone2
actions: Query org for customers with full_name ilike name, limit 2
If exactly 1 match and new phone not in (phone, phone2): UPDATE customers SET phone2=phone_norm, updated_at=now()
Return the existing customer record (with phone2 updated)
systemEffects: UPDATE customers.phone2 — silently overwrites any previous phone2 value
outputs: Existing customer record with phone2 set to new number
failureConditions: 0 or 2+ name matches → falls through to create new customer
New phone already stored → no update, return existing record
rules.evidence: same_name = ...ilike('full_name', name.strip()).limit(2)...; if len(same_name)==1: cust=same_name[0]; if phone_norm not in (cust.get('phone'), cust.get('phone2')): client.table('customers').update({'phone2': phone_norm, 'updated_at': 'now()'})(customers.py:118-137)

rules.name: Phone Normalization to E.164 (German Default)
rules.description: All phone numbers are normalized to E.164 before storage and lookup. Algorithm: (1) strip non-digits; (2) 00-prefix → strip 00, prepend +; (3) 0-prefix → strip trunk-0, prepend +49; (4) otherwise prepend +. Returns None for empty/whitespace input.
rules.purpose: Ensure that '0157 344 322 81', '+4915734432281', and '004915734432281' all resolve to the same canonical form so dedup works across formatting variants.
rules.trigger: Any path that reads or writes a phone number: create, update, CSV import, AI agent tools
validations: Non-digit characters stripped before logic
Must have at least one digit after stripping
actions: Strip non-digits
Apply prefix rules
Return E.164 string or None
failureConditions: Input empty or all non-digit → returns None
rules.evidence: def _to_e164(value, default_country='49'): digits=re.sub(r'\D','',value); if digits.startswith('00'): return '+'+digits[2:]; if digits.startswith('0'): return f'+{default_country}{digits[1:]}'; return '+'+digits (identify.py:24-47)

rules.name: Customer Number Auto-Generation (KI-NNNNNN)
rules.description: When a customer is created without an explicit customer_number, the system generates the next KI-NNNNNN number (zero-padded to 6 digits) by reading all existing KI- numbers for the org and taking max+1. Non-KI numbers (legacy/manual) are ignored in the sequence. Starting value is KI-000001.
rules.purpose: Provide a unique, system-tagged identifier that never collides with operator's own numbering (which may be plain integers).
rules.trigger: POST /api/customers (customer_number not provided), get_or_create_customer (always), CSV import rows without customer_number
validations: All existing customer_number values are read via fetch_all_rows (pages past 1000-row PostgREST cap)
Only values matching 'KI-' prefix with digit suffix advance the sequence
actions: Read all customers.customer_number for org via fetch_all_rows
Extract KI- sequence integers
Return KI-{max+1:06d} or KI-000001 if none
systemEffects: No immediate DB write — caller uses the returned number in the INSERT
failureConditions: Concurrent creates can race and mint the same number (no DB-level sequence constraint)
rules.evidence: def gen_customer_number(client, org_id): rows = fetch_all_rows(...customers...); seqs=[...ki_customer_seq...]; return f'KI-{(max(seqs)+1 if seqs else 1):06d}' (common.py:539-555)

rules.name: Customer identified_by Tracking
rules.description: Every customer record stores how it was first identified: 'phone' (created by AI agent/call path with a phone number), 'manual' (created via frontend form or AI with no phone), 'csv_import' (bulk CSV import). This is set at creation and never updated.
rules.purpose: Track acquisition channel for each customer for reporting and UI source badges.
actions: Set identified_by='phone' if phone_norm provided in get_or_create_customer
Set identified_by='manual' for frontend POST /api/customers
Set identified_by='csv_import' for CSV import path
rules.evidence: 'identified_by': 'phone' if phone_norm else 'manual' (customers.py:145); 'identified_by': 'manual' (routes/customers.py:463); 'identified_by': 'csv_import' (csv_import.py:459)

rules.name: phone2 Column for Secondary Phone Number
rules.description: Customers have a second phone field (phone2), intended for a mobile number when the primary phone is a landline. phone2 is stored in E.164 and indexed for dedup lookups. During CSV import, phone2 is skipped if equal to phone. The manual form labels it 'Telefon 2 (Mobil)'.
rules.purpose: Capture both a landline and a mobile for customers who have both (common for German Handwerker clients).
rules.trigger: POST /api/customers, PATCH /api/customers/{id}, POST /api/customers/import, get_or_create_customer (phone2 attachment on repeat callers)
validations: Normalized to E.164 before storage
Skipped in CSV if phone2 == phone
actions: Normalize phone2 via _to_e164
Store in customers.phone2
Partial index idx_customers_phone2 covers (org_id, phone2) where phone2 IS NOT NULL
systemEffects: customers.phone2 written on create or update
rules.evidence: ALTER TABLE customers ADD COLUMN phone2 text (0036); CREATE INDEX ON customers(org_id, phone2) WHERE phone2 IS NOT NULL (0050); 'phone2': phone2 if (phone2 and phone2 != phone) else None (csv_import.py:454-455)

rules.name: Dedup Guard on Manual Create — HTTP 409 on Collision
rules.description: The manual-create API (POST /api/customers) and update API (PATCH /api/customers/{id}) both run the shared find_existing_customer check before inserting/updating. On a collision, the request is rejected with HTTP 409, naming the existing customer's number. This prevents double-submit duplicates and race conditions.
rules.purpose: Block the form (and double-submit) from minting two customers on one mobile, since the old path inserted unconditionally.
preconditions: phone, phone2, or email provided in request
validations: find_existing_customer called for (phone, name, email)
If phone2 provided and primary check passes: check phone2+name too
On edit: match on own id is allowed; match on different id is 409
actions: Run find_existing_customer(client, org_id, phone=..., name=..., email=...)
If dup found and is not current record → raise HTTP 409
outputs: HTTP 409 with message including existing customer_number or name
rules.evidence: dup = find_existing_customer(...); if dup: raise HTTPException(status_code=409, detail=f'Es existiert bereits ein Kunde...(Kundennr. {num}).') (routes/customers.py:444-449)

rules.name: Soft Delete — Single and Bulk
rules.description: Customers are soft-deleted by setting status='deleted'. Both single delete (DELETE /api/customers/{id}) and bulk delete (POST /api/customers/bulk-delete) update status only. Deleted customers are excluded from all list, dedup, export, and detail queries via .neq('status','deleted').
rules.purpose: Preserve customer history (call records, inquiries) while removing the customer from active views.
preconditions: Customer must belong to calling user's org
validations: org_id filter ensures cross-tenant delete is impossible
actions: UPDATE customers SET status='deleted' WHERE org_id=... AND id IN (...)
failureConditions: No matching row in org → 404 (single) or deleted=0 (bulk)
rules.evidence: client.table('customers').update({'status': 'deleted'}).eq('org_id', user.org_id).eq('id', customer_id).execute() (routes/customers.py:591-596)

rules.name: Customer Type Classification
rules.description: Four customer types exist: new (Neukunde), regular (Stammkunde), supplier (Lieferant), property_management (Hausverwaltung). The default is 'new'. NULL is treated as 'new' in list counts. CSV import always sets 'regular' (Stammkunde). Manual create defaults to 'new' if not specified. No type transitions are enforced — user can set any value.
rules.purpose: Allow filtering and reporting by customer category (individual vs property manager vs supplier).
validations: No DB-level CHECK constraint — application accepts any string but frontend only offers 4 valid values
actions: Set customer_type on insert; allow update to any value
List query filters by customer_type if provided; count query treats NULL as 'new'
rules.evidence: _CUSTOMER_TYPES = ['new','regular','supplier','property_management'] (routes/customers.py:32). CSV: 'customer_type': 'regular' (csv_import.py:458). No DB CHECK constraint in 0006_customer_type_fields.sql.

rules.name: AI Agent identifyCustomer Tool — Lookup Priority Chain
rules.description: The AI agent's identifyCustomer tool resolves a caller to a customer record using a strict priority chain: (1) customer_number exact match (highest); (2) last_name ilike match or address search; (3) explicit phoneNumber; (4) Caller-ID (caller_number). If Caller-ID equals the org's own phone number, a FORWARDED_CALL status is returned instead of a lookup.
rules.purpose: Allow the AI agent to identify callers reliably across multiple signals, and detect forwarded internal calls.
rules.trigger: POST /api/elevenlabs/tools/identify-customer (called by ElevenLabs AI agent during a call)
preconditions: Org resolved via X-HeyKiki-Secret header or _agentId body field
inputs: payload: phoneNumber, customerNumber, address, lastName, caller_number (_callerNumber)
validations: Priority checked in order: customer_number first, then address/last_name, then phone
Forwarded call detected by matching Caller-ID to org.phone_number (digit-strip comparison)
actions: If customer_number: exact lookup → _resolve(rows)
If address or last_name: ilike search → _resolve(rows)
If Caller-ID == org phone: return FORWARDED_CALL status
Else: normalize phone to E.164, lookup on primary phone column only
outputs: Status EXISTING_CUSTOMER (with id, name, address, message in German) | NEW_CUSTOMER | MULTIPLE_CANDIDATES | FORWARDED_CALL
failureConditions: No identifier provided → returns NEW_CUSTOMER
Multiple matches → MULTIPLE_CANDIDATES with list of candidates
rules.evidence: Priority: if payload.customer_number → lookup; elif payload.address or payload.last_name → q.ilike; detect forwarded: _norm_phone(org_phone)==_norm_phone(caller); else normalize caller to E.164 → lookup on phone only (identify.py:92-157)

rules.name: identifyCustomer Phone Lookup Only Checks Primary phone Column
rules.description: The identifyCustomer tool's phone-based lookup only queries the primary phone column (not phone2). This is inconsistent with the dedup logic in find_existing_customer, which checks both phone and phone2. A customer whose primary phone was replaced and whose number is now only in phone2 will not be found by identify.
rules.purpose: UNVERIFIED OBSERVATION — the asymmetry appears to be a gap (the identify service predates phone2 support).
preconditions: Phone lookup path taken (no customer_number, no address/last_name)
actions: Normalize phone to E.164
Query .eq('phone', caller_norm or caller) — only primary phone column
failureConditions: Customer whose number is only in phone2 → not found → MULTIPLE_CANDIDATES or NEW_CUSTOMER
rules.evidence: .eq('phone', caller_norm or caller) — only primary phone column queried (identify.py:147-156). Contrast: find_existing_customer checks both phone and phone2 (customers.py:57-60).

rules.name: AI Agent updateCustomerData Tool — Field-Level Update
rules.description: The AI agent's updateCustomerData tool allows the agent to update name, email, phone, and/or address for a customer by customerId. Only provided (non-None) fields are updated. Phone is normalized to E.164 before storage. Returns success/failure with list of fields updated.
rules.purpose: Allow the AI agent to correct customer data during or after a call without requiring a human to manually edit.
preconditions: customer_id (customerId) must be provided in payload
validations: customer_id must be non-empty
At least one field must be provided
Phone normalized to E.164 if provided
Address stored as {'raw': value} JSONB
actions: Build fields dict from non-None payload values
UPDATE customers WHERE org_id=... AND id=customer_id
Return {success, updatedFields, message}
outputs: {success: True, updatedFields: [...], message: 'Kundendaten erfolgreich aktualisiert.'} or {success: False, message: ...}
failureConditions: customer_id missing → {success:False}
No fields provided → {success:False}
Customer not found in org → {success:False, message:'Kunde nicht gefunden.'}
rules.evidence: if not payload.customer_id: return {success:False,...}; if not fields: return {success:False,...}; UPDATE customers WHERE org_id AND id (customers.py:153-197)

rules.name: Post-Call Automatic Customer Creation
rules.description: After a completed AI call, the post_call webhook runs get_or_create_customer using Caller-ID first, then AI-extracted data_collection fields (customer_phone, customer_name, customer_address). This ensures every completed call is linked to a customer record without manual intervention. 'Unknown'/'anonymous' Caller-IDs are filtered out.
rules.purpose: Ensure no call is ever unlinked from a customer record, even for new callers.
rules.trigger: POST /api/calls/post-call webhook (called by N8N after ElevenLabs call ends)
preconditions: Caller has a non-empty, non-anonymous Caller-ID OR AI extracted a customer_phone/customer_name
validations: _ok() filter: non-empty and not in ('', 'unbekannt', 'keiner', 'anonymous')
Caller-ID preferred; AI-extracted phone as fallback
actions: If link_phone or dc_name: call get_or_create_customer(org_id, phone=link_phone, name=dc_name, address=dc_addr)
Attach customer_id to call record
systemEffects: May INSERT new customer row; always links customer_id to the call
failureConditions: No phone and no name → no customer link (customer_id remains None on call)
rules.evidence: link_phone = caller_number if _ok(caller_number) else None; if not link_phone and _ok(dc_values.get('customer_phone')): link_phone = dc_values['customer_phone']; customer = get_or_create_customer(org_id, phone=link_phone, ...) (post_call.py:343-356)

rules.name: Address Storage as JSONB (Two Shapes)
rules.description: The address column is JSONB and accepts two shapes: {raw: string} (manual entry, stored verbatim) and {street, postal_code, city} (CSV import with structured mapping). A generated column address_text (migration 0051) flattens both shapes for sorting using IMMUTABLE Postgres operators. format_address() in Python handles both shapes consistently.
rules.purpose: Support both free-text address entry (frontend form) and structured address import (CSV) without requiring a schema change or migration of existing data.
rules.trigger: Customer create/update (raw shape), CSV import (structured shape), or read for sorting/display
validations: Manual create stores {'raw': value}
CSV stores {street, postal_code, city}
address_text generated column uses coalesce over raw vs concatenated structured fields
actions: Store address JSONB in appropriate shape
address_text auto-computed for sorting
rules.evidence: def _addr(value): return {'raw': value} if value else None (routes/customers.py:29-30). CSV: address = {'street': street, 'postal_code': plz, 'city': city} (csv_import.py:435-436). generated column in 0051 uses coalesce(nullif(address->>'raw',...), concat of structured fields)

rules.name: Customer List: Paginated, Filterable, Sortable
rules.description: GET /api/customers returns a paginated, filterable, sortable customer list with per-type counts and per-customer enrichment counts (inquiries, appointments, photos, documents). Sort columns are whitelisted (injection guard). Unknown sort column defaults to created_at desc. NULL customer_type is bucketed as 'new' in type counts.
rules.purpose: Support the Kontakte UI with server-side pagination (avoids loading thousands of rows) and filter tabs with accurate counts.
validations: limit clamped 1-500
sort_by whitelisted; unknown → created_at
address sort_by aliased to address_text column
actions: Run page query and 5 count queries concurrently via asyncio.gather
Fetch enrichment counts (inquiries, appointments, docs) concurrently via run_in_threadpool+fetch_all_rows
outputs: {customers: [...], total: N, type_counts: {all, new, regular, supplier, property_management}}
rules.evidence: _SORT_COLUMNS = {'created_at','full_name','customer_number','phone','address_text'} (routes/customers.py:39); type_counts via parallel asyncio.gather (routes/customers.py:116-121); enrichment via run_in_threadpool+fetch_all_rows (routes/customers.py:133-167)

rules.name: Customer CSV Export
rules.description: GET /api/customers/export exports all customers matching the current view (search + type filter + sort) as a semicolon-delimited, UTF-8-BOM CSV file. The export pages past PostgREST's 1000-row limit. Address is flattened to a string for export (raw or structured). Type and source are rendered as German labels.
rules.purpose: Allow operators to export their customer database for use in external tools (Excel, ERP).
actions: Page through all matching customers in batches of 1000
Write semicolon CSV with UTF-8 BOM
Render customer_type as German label; identified_by as German label; address as flattened string
rules.evidence: buf.write('\ufeff') # UTF-8 BOM; w = csv.writer(buf, delimiter=';'); paged loop with batch<1000 break (routes/customers.py:364-388)

rules.name: CSV Import: Email Validation and Phone Salvage
rules.description: During CSV import, a column mapped to 'email' is validated with a strict regex (local@domain.tld, no spaces). Values that fail email validation but look like phone numbers (only phone-ish chars, ≥5 digits) are salvaged into the phone or phone2 slot if available. Pure junk values that are neither email nor phone are dropped with a correction log entry.
rules.purpose: German ERP exports routinely misplace phone numbers in the Mail column; this prevents junk data from entering the email field and recovers the actual phone number.
preconditions: Row has a value in the column mapped to 'email'
validations: _valid_email: must match ^[^@\s]+@[^@\s]+\.[^@\s]+$
_looks_like_phone: only phone-ish chars, ≥5 digits
actions: If raw_email fails email regex AND looks like phone: salvage to phone or phone2 slot (if free)
If raw_email fails email regex AND does NOT look like phone: drop (log as junk_email_dropped)
systemEffects: corrections list populated with phone_salvaged_from_email or junk_email_dropped entries
rules.evidence: _valid_email: return v if _EMAIL_RE.match(v) else None; _looks_like_phone: re.fullmatch(r'[\d\s+\-/().]+',v) and sum(c.isdigit()...)>=5; salvaged into slot='phone' or 'phone2' (csv_import.py:89-105, 385-400)

rules.name: CSV Import: Address Extraction from Notes Column
rules.description: If a customer row has no address from the dedicated address columns, the CSV importer scans the notes/Bemerkung column for a PLZ-anchored street address pattern (Street, 12345 City). If found, the extracted address is stored in the structured {street, postal_code, city} shape and logged as a correction.
rules.purpose: German ERP exports sometimes misplace addresses in the Bemerkung field; this recovers the address without requiring manual re-mapping.
rules.trigger: POST /api/customers/import, per row where address is None after standard column extraction
preconditions: address is None after mapping street/postal_code/city columns
validations: Pattern: (.+?)[,\s]+(\d{5})\s+([A-Za-zÄÖÜäöüß][\wÄÖÜäöüß .\-]+?)\s*$
Must match a 5-digit German PLZ — plain notes without address are left untouched
actions: Scan each line of notes for address pattern
If match: set address = {street, postal_code, city}
Log correction as action='address_from_notes'
systemEffects: address field populated in INSERT payload
failureConditions: Notes without a PLZ-anchored address are left unchanged
rules.evidence: _ADDR_RE = re.compile(r'(.+?)[,\s]+(\d{5})\s+...'); if address is None and notes: found=extract_address(notes); if found: address=found; corrections.append({action:'address_from_notes',...}) (csv_import.py:127-147, 440-447)

rules.name: CSV Import: Column Type Detection from Cell Data
rules.description: The import preview API analyzes each column's cell values (not headers) to detect the dominant content type. Type precedence: email > postal_code (exactly 5 digits) > bare-number-not-starting-0 (customer_number) > phone-ish chars (mobile or landline) > street token > alpha-only (person_name if distinct ≥60%, else city). Header name hints break ties. Columns with mixed mobile+landline are flagged as mixed_phone.
rules.purpose: Prevent a phone-number column from being proposed as the Email or Address target by reading actual cell content rather than column headers.
validations: Confidence = fraction of cells matching dominant type
actions: Parse CSV; for each column, call detect_column_type on up to sample_size non-empty cells; suggest_mapping picks best column per field by content type then header hint score
outputs: {headers, columns: {type, confidence, samples, mixed_phone}, suggested_mapping, row_count}
rules.evidence: def _value_type(v): if _valid_email(v): return 'email'; if re.fullmatch(r'\d{5}',v): return 'postal_code'; if v.isdigit() and not v.startswith('0'): return 'number'; if _looks_like_phone(v): return 'mobile'/'landline'... (csv_import.py:162-181)

rules.name: CSV Import: Idempotent Dedup (In-File + DB)
rules.description: CSV import deduplication is idempotent: re-running the same file does not insert new rows. Dedup tracks seen_emails, seen_mobiles, and seen_landline_names across both the existing DB rows (fetched in full via fetch_all_rows, paged past 1000-row cap) and earlier rows in the same file. Each row gets a result: imported / skipped_duplicate / error. CSV import sets customer_type='regular'.
rules.purpose: Allow safe re-import of the same customer export without creating duplicates.
validations: All existing customers read via fetch_all_rows (not capped)
Same mobile/email/landline+name seen earlier in file also deduplicated
actions: Read ALL existing customers for org via fetch_all_rows
Build seen_emails, seen_mobiles, seen_landline_names from existing + earlier-in-file rows
Batch insert in chunks of 500
outputs: {total, imported, skipped_duplicate, errors, corrected, results, corrections}
failureConditions: Row with no name/email/phone → status='error', reason='Kein Name/E-Mail/Telefon'
rules.evidence: existing = fetch_all_rows(...customers...); seen_emails: set; seen_mobiles: set; seen_landline_names: dict; _register(email_l, numbers, name_l) after each imported row; _CHUNK=500 batch insert (csv_import.py:333-467)

rules.name: Org-Scoped Multi-Tenancy for All Customer Operations
rules.description: All customer read and write operations filter by org_id derived from the authenticated user's JWT. RLS policies in Supabase additionally enforce org isolation at the DB layer (defense-in-depth). For AI agent tool endpoints, org_id is resolved from the X-HeyKiki-Secret header (per-org shared secret stored in org_secrets) or the _agentId body field (matched to organizations.elevenlabs_agent_id).
rules.purpose: Strict tenant isolation — one org can never read or modify another org's customer records.
inputs: Bearer JWT (frontend routes) or X-HeyKiki-Secret / _agentId (AI tool routes)
validations: require_org: user.org_id must be non-None; org must not be disabled_at
resolve_tool_org: looks up org_secrets.secret or organizations.elevenlabs_agent_id
actions: Every query includes .eq('org_id', org_id)
failureConditions: No org_id → 403 Forbidden
Disabled org → 403 (unless super_admin)
Unknown secret or agent_id → 401
rules.evidence: require_org: if not user.org_id: raise 403 (deps.py:66-71); RLS policy: create policy customers_org_all on customers for all using (org_id = auth_org_id()) (0001_init_schema.sql:274-290); resolve_tool_org: lookup via org_secrets or elevenlabs_agent_id (deps.py:154-206)

rules.name: Customer Detail — Enriched With Full Activity History
rules.description: GET /api/customers/{id} returns the full customer record plus all related inquiries, appointments, calls, and cost_estimates fetched concurrently. Inquiries are enriched with per-inquiry call_count, open_count, and last_activity_at computed in Python. Cases (Fälle) are included (non-archived, linked via customer_id). Projects (top-layer) are included if any case has a project_id.
rules.purpose: Provide the customer detail page with a complete 360-degree view of the customer's history in one round-trip.
validations: org_id filter ensures cross-tenant access is impossible
actions: Fetch customer row (*)
Concurrently fetch inquiries, appointments, calls, cost_estimates via run_parallel
Compute call_count/open_count/last_activity_at per inquiry in Python
Fetch non-archived cases for customer; include project roll-up if case.project_id exists
outputs: Full customer object with inquiries[], appointments[], calls[], cost_estimates[], cases[], projects[]
failureConditions: Customer not in org → 404
rules.evidence: customer['inquiries'], customer['appointments'], customer['calls'], customer['cost_estimates'] = run_parallel(_inq, _appt, _calls, _kvas) (routes/customers.py:229). Cases: .neq('status','archived') (routes/customers.py:270)
